tasks.//worker-options: --unstable-worker-options is required wherever the server runs: source plugins poll inside Workers whose permissions are scoped down via `deno: { permissions }`, which Deno 2.9 still gates behind this flag. Note api's --allow-net is NOT scoped to 127.0.0.1 anymore for the same reason the desktop task's isn't: plugin workers can only be granted a subset of the host's net access.
tasks.//check: main.ts is deliberately absent: Deno.BrowserWindow only exists in the desktop lib, so plain `deno check` cannot see it. `deno desktop` typechecks the entrypoint itself at launch.
tasks.check: deno check server/ cli/ mcp/ && npm run check
tasks.//permissions: --allow-net is unscoped (not just 127.0.0.1) because the Phase 6 pollers call out to GitHub/Linear, and their API host is user-configurable (GitHub Enterprise, self-hosted). --allow-run is scoped to `open`: the openExternal/revealInFinder bindings, nothing else.
tasks.desktop: npm run build && deno desktop --unstable-worker-options --allow-env --allow-read --allow-write --allow-net --allow-run=open --include .deno-deploy --include server main.ts
tasks.build:app: npm run build && deno desktop --unstable-worker-options --allow-env --allow-read --allow-write --allow-net --allow-run=open --include .deno-deploy --include server --output out/dev-stream.app main.ts && codesign --force --sign - out/dev-stream.app
tasks.//build:app: --include is required: main.ts dynamically loads .deno-deploy and server/. Permissions are baked in at compile time. The trailing codesign is NOT redundant: deno desktop 2.9.2 copies the icon into Contents/Resources AFTER signing, which invalidates the signature ('a sealed resource is missing or invalid').
tasks.//install: Build and install locally (macOS): the CLI into ~/.local/bin (already on PATH) and the desktop app into /Applications. Run with sudo (sudo deno task install) since /Applications is root-owned. Re-run after changes to refresh the installed copies. The compiled CLI is self-contained, so its absolute path ~/.local/bin/dev-stream is a stable target for an MCP client command.
tasks.install: deno task build:cli && deno task build:app && mkdir -p $HOME/.local/bin && mkdir -p /Applications && rm -f $HOME/.local/bin/dev-stream && cp out/dev-stream $HOME/.local/bin/dev-stream && rm -rf /Applications/dev-stream.app && cp -R out/dev-stream.app /Applications/dev-stream.app && echo '' && echo 'Installed:' && echo '  CLI  ~/.local/bin/dev-stream  (on PATH)' && echo '  App  /Applications/dev-stream.app'
tasks.build:app:release: npm run build && deno desktop --unstable-worker-options --allow-env --allow-read --allow-write --allow-net --allow-run=open --include .deno-deploy --include server --output out/dev-stream.app main.ts && codesign --force --sign - out/dev-stream.app && codesign --verify --verbose out/dev-stream.app
tasks.//build:all: Cross-compile the CLI. The .app itself needs a macOS host (codesign + bundle), so the desktop matrix is macOS-only here.
tasks.build:all: deno compile --allow-net=127.0.0.1 --allow-read --allow-write --allow-env --allow-run=git --target aarch64-apple-darwin  --output out/dist/dev-stream-macos-arm64 cli/main.ts && deno compile --allow-net=127.0.0.1 --allow-read --allow-write --allow-env --allow-run=git --target x86_64-apple-darwin   --output out/dist/dev-stream-macos-x64 cli/main.ts && deno compile --allow-net=127.0.0.1 --allow-read --allow-write --allow-env --allow-run=git --target x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu --output out/dist/dev-stream-linux-x64 cli/main.ts && deno compile --allow-net=127.0.0.1 --allow-read --allow-write --allow-env --allow-run=git --target aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu --output out/dist/dev-stream-linux-arm64 cli/main.ts && deno compile --allow-net=127.0.0.1 --allow-read --allow-write --allow-env --allow-run=git --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc --output out/dist/dev-stream-windows-x64.exe cli/main.ts
tasks.//build:app:release: --compress is deliberately absent: on Deno 2.9.2 it breaks codesign outright, and even after a manual deep sign the self-extracting stub cannot find its own inner binary. It would take the app from 156MB to 32MB, so revisit when deno desktop stabilises. Swap `-` for a Developer ID identity to distribute.
